title: Copy the freshly built herdr-go binary into ~/.local/bin so it runs directly from a terminal
action: Copy target/release/herdr-go (already built by dev-deploy.sh, currently what herdr-go-dev.service runs) to ~/.local/bin/herdr-go with mode 0755, mirroring exactly what install.sh's `install -m 0755 "$tmp_dir/$asset/herdr-go" "$BIN_DIR/herdr-go"` does for the published-binary install path (BIN_DIR defaults to $HOME/.local/bin). This only adds a terminal-invocable copy of the binary the user already has running as a service; it does not touch the running herdr-go-dev.service, its config, or its data.

truths: ~/.local/bin/herdr-go exists, is executable, and running `herdr-go --help`/`herdr-go --version` (or equivalent) from a shell with ~/.local/bin on PATH succeeds without 'command not found'
The running herdr-go-dev.service (still ExecStart=.../target/release/herdr-go) is unaffected
prohibitions: Do not stop, restart, or reconfigure herdr-go-dev.service

verify: test -x "$HOME/.local/bin/herdr-go" && cmp -s "$HOME/.local/bin/herdr-go" target/release/herdr-go && systemctl --user is-active herdr-go-dev.service && echo OK

trace.outcome: Copied target/release/herdr-go to ~/.local/bin/herdr-go (mode 0755), matching install.sh's install step; herdr-go now runs directly from a shell. herdr-go-dev.service untouched, still active.
trace.verify_command: test -x ~/.local/bin/herdr-go && cmp -s ~/.local/bin/herdr-go target/release/herdr-go && systemctl --user is-active herdr-go-dev.service && echo OK
